batches.prompt:
I'm fact-checking an AI job automation risk dataset. For each job below, assess the AI automation risk on a 0-100 scale where:
- 0-15 = Raw (very safe from AI)
- 16-30 = Rare (mostly safe)
- 31-45 = Lightly Toasted (some tasks at risk)
- 46-60 = Half Baked (significant portions automatable)
- 61-75 = Well Done (majority of tasks at risk)
- 76-90 = Extra Crispy (heavily automatable)
- 91-100 = Fully Cooked (almost entirely replaceable)

Consider CURRENT AI capabilities as of 2026 (LLMs, computer vision, robotics, coding agents, etc). Not theoretical future — what's real now.

I'll show our current score. Tell me if you agree or disagree.

IMPORTANT: Format each response as exactly one line per job:
job_id | your_score | AGREE or DISAGREE | brief reason

Jobs to assess:
1. arborist — "Arborist" (our score: 4/100, 6% automatable)
2. surgeon — "Surgeon" (our score: 4/100, 5% automatable)
3. firefighter — "Firefighter" (our score: 5/100, 6% automatable)
4. emergency-medicine-doctor — "Emergency Medicine Doctor" (our score: 6/100, 7% automatable)
5. massage-therapist — "Massage Therapist" (our score: 6/100, 7% automatable)
6. plumber — "Plumber" (our score: 6/100, 8% automatable)
7. roofer — "Roofer" (our score: 6/100, 7% automatable)
8. electrician — "Electrician" (our score: 7/100, 9% automatable)
9. glazier — "Glazier" (our score: 7/100, 8% automatable)
10. midwife — "Midwife" (our score: 7/100, 8% automatable)
11. tiler — "Tiler" (our score: 7/100, 8% automatable)
12. anaesthesiologist — "Anaesthesiologist" (our score: 8/100, 10% automatable)
13. bricklayer — "Bricklayer" (our score: 8/100, 9% automatable)
14. carpenter — "Carpenter" (our score: 8/100, 10% automatable)
15. dog-groomer — "Dog Groomer" (our score: 8/100, 9% automatable)

results.reason: Physical tree work, climbing, hazard assessment require dexterity beyond 2026 capabilities
AI assists imaging/planning, robotic surgery needs human oversight for precision and complications
Unpredictable environments, physical endurance, real-time decisions exceed current robotics
AI aids triage/diagnosis but hands-on procedures and rapid judgment remain human-essential
Tactile feedback, customized pressure, client interaction not replicable by current robotics
Tight-space diagnostics, improvised repairs, physical maneuvering challenge current AI/robotics
Heights, weather variability, material handling demand human agility beyond 2026 tech
Complex wiring, safety judgments, troubleshooting in varied settings resist full automation
Precise glass handling, cutting, installation on uneven surfaces need human finesse
Emotional support, physical delivery assistance, real-time maternal monitoring require human empathy
Surface prep, pattern layout, grout adjustments involve craft not automated by current AI
AI monitors vitals but real-time airway management and crisis response demand expert intervention
Mortar mixing, leveling, structural adaptations exceed robotic precision in real-world builds
Custom measurements, on-site adjustments, woodworking creativity beyond current robotics
Animal handling, behavior reading, gentle grooming need human touch and intuition
